edges.description: Combined NEU1/GLB1 deficiency impairs glycoconjugate degradation, so substrates accumulate.
edges.source: Cathepsin A (PPCA) Deficiency and Secondary Combined NEU1/GLB1 Deficiency

edges.description: Lysosomal storage drives progressive multisystem and neurological disease.

edges.target: Cathepsin A (PPCA) Deficiency and Secondary Combined NEU1/GLB1 Deficiency

meta.association: Biallelic CTSA variants abolishing protective protein/cathepsin A (PPCA)

evidence.reference_title: Galactosialidosis: review and analysis of CTSA gene mutations.
evidence.snippet:
Mutations in the CTSA gene, that encodes the protective
protein/cathepsin A or PPCA, lead to the secondary deficiency of β-galactosidase
(GLB1) and neuraminidase 1 (NEU1), causing the lysosomal storage disorder
galactosialidosis (GS).

nodes.description: Biallelic CTSA variants abolish protective protein/cathepsin A (PPCA), required to assemble, stabilize, and activate the lysosomal multienzyme complex containing neuraminidase-1 and beta-galactosidase. PPCA loss secondarily inactivates both enzymes.
nodes.id: Cathepsin A (PPCA) Deficiency and Secondary Combined NEU1/GLB1 Deficiency

evidence.reference_title: Galactosialidosis: review and analysis of CTSA gene mutations.
evidence.snippet:
Mutations in the CTSA gene, that encodes the protective
protein/cathepsin A or PPCA, lead to the secondary deficiency of β-galactosidase
(GLB1) and neuraminidase 1 (NEU1), causing the lysosomal storage disorder
galactosialidosis (GS).
Genetic defects in NEU1 or in its protective protein cathepsin A (PPCA, CTSA) cause the lysosomal storage diseases sialidosis and galactosialidosis

nodes.description: Storage-cell injury produces progressive multisystem disease (skeletal, ocular, cardiac, renal, and neurological), most severe in the early-infantile form, completing the lysosomal-storage cascade.

evidence.snippet: the rare infantile form of GS

nodes.description: Secondary combined deficiency of neuraminidase-1 and beta-galactosidase impairs glycoprotein and glycolipid catabolism, causing intralysosomal accumulation and urinary excretion of sialylated oligosaccharides and glycopeptides.

evidence.reference_title: Galactosialidosis: review and analysis of CTSA gene mutations.
evidence.snippet:
causing the lysosomal storage disorder
galactosialidosis (GS)
